channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Prosecutors: Trump 'resorted to crimes' after losing 2020 election in failed bid to cling to power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump “resorted to crimes” after losing the 2020 election, federal prosecutors said in a court filing unsealed Wednesday that argues that the former president disregarded the advice of his vice president and other aides and is not entitled to immunity from prosecution over h
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cybersecurity head says there's no chance a foreign adversary can change US election results
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Security for America's election systems has become so robust that Russia, Iran or any other foreign adversary will not be able to alter the outcome of this year’s presidential race, the head of the nation’s cybersecurity agency said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: David Valadao, Rudy Salas face off in 22nd Congressional District Debate
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET/KGPE) -- The two candidates seeking election to California's 22nd Congressional District will face off tonight. The debate will take place at the KGET news studio in Bakersfield.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, NewsNation

item.title: Biden won't support a strike on Iran nuclear sites as Israel weighs response to Iran missile attack
item.description: JOINT BASE ANDREWS, Md. (AP) — President Joe Biden said Wednesday he will not support an Israeli strike on sites related to Tehran’s nuclear program in response to Iran's missile attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voters have largely positive response to exchange between Vance and Walz on shootings
item.description: Voters from all groups had a mostly positive response to Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz's exchange on school shootings and gun control during the debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris dodges answering whether athletes should stand during national anthem in unearthed interview
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris did not answer directly whether athletes should stand during the national anthem, an unearthed media interview in 2016 shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance oozed confidence compared to jittery Walz, body language expert says: 'Captain of the ship'
item.description: Sen. JD Vance, R-Ohio, and Democratic Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz's body language sharply contrasted with one another at Tuesday's vice presidential debate, a body language expert says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tim Walz says 'most of us want to solve' migrant crisis: voters react
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz received steady support from independent and Democratic debate watchers during his remarks on immigration, except when he bashed former President Trump on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania town grapples with Trump assassination attempt ahead of his return
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Donald Trump is going back to Butler, where the world last saw him pump his fist and beseech followers to “fight,” even as blood streaked his face from a would-be assassin's bullet.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voters react to JD Vance claiming he and Donald Trump support 'clean air, clean water'
item.description: JD Vance said at the CBS Vice Presidential Debate that he and Donald Trump support "clean air, clean water," prompting mixed reactions from voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trio of crises loom over final the campaign's final stretch
item.description: In a debate that evoked a calmer era in American politics, Tim Walz and JD Vance went after each other’s running mates Tuesday and sought to shore up their campaigns’ vulnerabilities at a time of renewed fears of a regional war in the Middle East and sadness over devastation from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Jersey Dem House candidate says she is 'not super worried' about biological men in women's locker rooms
item.description: A New Jersey Democrat and former women's basketball player running for Congress said she is "not super worried" about biological men being in women's locker rooms
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In South Texas congressional race, Monica De La Cruz and Michelle Vallejo spar over health care
item.description: The topic is playing a major role in one of Texas’ only competitive congressional races this year as the competitors debate Medicare, abortion and expanding coverage.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jasper Scherer, KPRC 2

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ and MARIANA MARTÍNEZ BARBA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In VP debate, Walz acknowledges he wasn’t in China during Tiananmen uprising
item.description: APM Reports, the investigations and documentaries unit of MPR News, first reported that Walz’s claim during a 2014 congressional hearing on the 25th anniversary of the massacre didn’t square up with media accounts in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Tom Scheck, Curtis Gilbert, Caspar von Au, and Forest Hunt, MPR News

item.title: Walz and Vance go in depth on policy while attacking each other’s running mates in VP debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In a debate that evoked a calmer era in American politics, Tim Walz and JD Vance on Tuesday went after each other's running mates and sought to shore up their campaigns' vulnerabilities at a time of renewed fears of a regional war in the Middle East and sadness over devastation from
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, CHRIS MEGERIAN, WILL WEISSSERT and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Best and worst debate moments for Vance, Walz
item.description: Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) engaged in a policy-focused debate Tuesday, civilly sparring on immigration, abortion and the economy, among other issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voters react to Gov. Tim Walz claiming abortion is a 'basic human right'
item.description: Fox News Digital's dial group made up of independent, Democratic and Republican voters differed on Democratic Gov. Tim Walz's comments on abortion in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris and Trump are tested by the Mideast, Helene and the port strike in the campaign's final weeks
item.description: Hurricane Helene, the growing conflict in the Mideast and a dockworkers strike are looming over the final weeks of the American presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Zeke Miller And Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Prosecutors say they may bring additional charges against New York City mayor and indict others
item.description: Federal prosecutors say they might bring additional charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams and indict others in the corruption case against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some of the most notable quotes from the JD Vance-Tim Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: The debate between Ohio Senator JD Vance and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz featured an often orderly, policy-focused and even civil dialogue between the two men seeking to serve as the next president’s second in command.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida Republican 'absolutely certain' there will be supplemental to address Helene aid
item.description: Rep. Kat Cammack (R-Fla.) said Monday she’s “absolutely certain” Congress will consider a supplemental aid package to provide additional disaster relief to areas devastated by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Tim Walz’s experience in China could help him as veep, but he barely mentions it as a candidate
item.description: When Gov. Tim Walz first ran for Congress in 2006, his campaign proudly touted his extensive experience in China. In fact, earlier in his political career, he occasionally exaggerated it. But now that he’s running for vice president, Walz barely mentions China.
item.dc:creator: Forest Hunt, Caspar von Au, and Curtis Gilbert, MPR News

item.title: VIDEO: Donald Trump tosses chicken tenders into crowd at college football game
item.description: Former President Donald Trump surprised college students on Saturday in Alabama with an unusual gift for football fans.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Understanding the cat lore of the 2024 presidential election
item.description: The “cat lady” tagline took off this year as an insult and an accolade — now it’s a mainstay in the presidential campaigns and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PIX Panel discusses historic indictment against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – History was made in New York City this week when Eric Adams became the city’s first sitting mayor to be indicted on federal charges. A federal indictment charged Adams with bribery, campaign finance and conspiracy offenses.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Prop. 36 faces intense review as North County residents gather for informative session
item.description: Voters have a lot to consider this upcoming election when it comes to Proposition 36, a statewide ballot measure that seeks a tougher approach on crime.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5
